title: dicranopteris tetraphylla
intro: Dicranopteris tetraphylla, also known as the leather fern or tala, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Matoniaceae. This plant is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Dicranopteris tetraphylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Southeast Asian medicine to treat a range of ailments.
